The wi_skills plan is marginally more detailed (9417 bytes vs 7670 bytes), with more explicit contact times (e.g., '15 minutes minimum' for hood filters, '30 minutes' for oven), a more granular phase breakdown, and richer compliance language (explicitly citing OSHA, EPA, and food service regulations). The difference is smaller than anticipated by the test design — the baseline agent without the skill still produced a commercially viable, inspection-ready plan. Overall, wi_skills produces a slightly more authoritative and detailed document, but both are functionally equivalent for the cleaning crew's purposes.

However, explicit separate mop head rules for different floor types are less detailed than wi_skills. The guide does mention color-coded cloths as a tool-separation rule.

items.criterion: Does the weekly schedule assign distinct tasks to specific frequencies (daily, weekly, or periodic) and cover all major zones in the office — rather than listing a flat undifferentiated set of cleaning tasks? Pass: at least two different frequency tiers are represented (e.g., daily high-touch disinfection vs. weekly deep clean of break room), and all major zones are addressed. Fail: schedule is a single flat list without frequency differentiation or misses major zones.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills weekly schedule clearly differentiates Daily Base Tasks (every service day), Weekly Rotation (Monday through Friday focus areas), and Monthly Tasks (leather conditioning, carpet interim cleaning, stone/wood review). All major zones are covered across the three frequencies, satisfying both frequency differentiation and zone coverage requirements.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills weekly schedule differentiates 'Daily Every Visit', day-specific weekly tasks (Monday through Friday), and 'As Needed' tasks. All major zones including marble, hardwood, carpet, leather, stainless steel, glass, and restrooms are addressed. At least two frequency tiers (daily vs. weekly) are clearly represented with comprehensive zone coverage.

utility_details.judge_error: No errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both agents completed successfully and produced the required output files.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive cleaning documentation that satisfies all seven evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills demonstrates marginally superior output quality in several areas: (1) It includes an explicit, dedicated 'Recommended Tool Separation' section with named color-coded cloths and separate mop head rules per floor type — a more actionable and complete implementation of J6. (2) The wi_skills surface guide is more structured with a distinct 'Critical Damage Prevention' section prominently addressing both documented incidents at the top of the document. (3) The wi_skills weekly schedule includes a formal 'Monthly Tasks' tier, providing clearer three-tier frequency differentiation (daily/weekly/monthly) compared to wo_skills' 'As Needed' category. (4) The wi_skills guide explicitly references the office materials map context file, demonstrating deeper integration with provided resources. The wo_skills output is also competent and passes all evaluation items, but wi_skills edges ahead in structural clarity, explicit tool segregation rules, and the formalized monthly maintenance tier. Overall: wi_skills is slightly more effective.
